content.full_text:
Peace Officers Research Association of California

CENTRAL COAST CHAPTER, 880 B NORTH FIRST STREET, SAN JOSE, CALIFORNIA 95112

THE OBJECTIVE OF THIS ASSOCIATION IS TO UNITE ALL PERSONS WITHIN ITS JURISDICTION FOR THEIR ECONOMIC, PROFESSIONAL AND SOCIAL ADVANCEMENT. IT SHALL BE THE AIM OF THIS ASSOCIATION TO COLLECT, STUDY, STANDARDIZE, SUMMARIZE AND DISTRIBUTE FACTUAL DATA IN ORDER TO PROMOTE THE PROFESSIONAL QUALIFICATIONS AND STANDING OF PEACE OFFICERS, TO STIMULATE MUTUAL COOPERATION BETWEEN LAW ENFORCEMENT AGENCIES, TO SECURE FOR ALL PEACE OFFICERS ADEQUATE COMPENSATION FOR THEIR PROFESSIONAL DUTIES AND TO IMPROVE CONDITIONS OF EMPLOYMENT.

January 26, 1978

A-78-01-025

F.P.P.C.
1100 K Street
Sacramento, Ca 95814

Dear Sirs:

We have just realized we have received in excess of $500.00 for political contributions last year. We have never in the past gone anywhere near that amount.

We understand we need a committee number as receiving monies for that purpose. We just now found that out. We have not had a committee number in the past, not realizing we needed one.

We hope this letter will rectify the error.

Thank you.

Sincerely yours,

O.J. Holt
O. J. Holt, President
Central Coast Chapter, PORAC

OJH:ms

O.J. Holt, President, Peace Officers Research Association—Advised by phone (Feb. 14, 1978) that they were not a committee because they had only raised approximately $250. However, they did function as an intermediary for an additional $300 which has been earmarked by the contributor for a specific candidate, and I advised Mr. Holt to so inform the recipient in writing.
